segments.title: Modern warship command preference question
segments.question: What class or specific vessel would you like to be captain of today, and why that vessel?
segments.answerShort: The answer considers trying a Type 055 for handling insight, then favors a Type 45 or Iver Huitfeldt to examine Type 83 lessons and StanFlex value.
segments.summary: A Bruships 243 Q&A asks which modern warship Dr. Clarke would captain and why.
segments.body: In this exchange, Dr. Clarke answers a concrete modern-navy prompt. He links personal command preference to practical research value: a Type 055 would test reported handling, an Iver Huitfeldt would expose StanFlex potential, and a Type 45 would inform thinking about future Type 83 requirements.

evidence.note: A viewer asks which modern vessel Dr. Clarke would captain, and the answer compares Type 055, Iver Huitfeldt, Type 45, StanFlex, and Type 83 implications.
The follow-up clarifies the Type 31/Iver Huitfeldt relationship and why StanFlex was not actually adopted.
